response: {"purpose":"Figure 1a illustrates the step-by-step experimental workflow of eCLIP-seq, a method designed to identify RNA-binding protein (RBP) target sites with improved specificity and efficiency. The schematic walks through each key stage — UV crosslinking, RNase I digestion, immunoprecipitation, adapter ligation, gel electrophoresis, and paired-end library preparation — highlighting technical innovations such as the inline barcode on the RNA adapter and the random-mer sequence used for PCR duplicate removal. A critical feature emphasized is that read 2 captures the 5′ end of the original RNA fragment, which frequently marks reverse transcriptase termination at the crosslink site, enabling precise mapping of protein–RNA contact points. This figure matters because it establishes the methodological foundation for all subsequent analyses in the paper, demonstrating how eCLIP-seq addresses limitations of earlier CLIP approaches to yield more reliable and reproducible RBP binding data.","title":"eCLIP-seq workflow for stabilizing and sequencing RBP-RNA interactions"}
